page: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but features a high-speed network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The server also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in ray tracing, image processing, and cryptographic operations, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES. Memory bandwidth is strong, achieving 335.16 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 129.5 GB/sec for write operations. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50% range.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high resource density, c6in.metal offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. The combination of 128 dedicated vCPUs and 200 Gbps network bandwidth makes it highly suitable for network-intensive compute tasks, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput database operations, and static web serving. It is also capable of handling moderate LLM inference tasks, though text generation speeds are average.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 256 GB of DDR4 memory and a 200 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPUs. The server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU benchmarks, alongside strong memory bandwidth of up to 335.16 GB/sec for large-block reads. It excels in parallel processing tasks, such as ray tracing, image processing, and cryptographic operations, while single-core performance remains average. This bare-metal instance is highly efficient for network-intensive compute workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput database operations requiring direct hardware access.

256 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth.
